docs.title: Dot Trace Generator
docs.text:
dot_trace_generator.py takes a DOT file or JSON and outputs SPOT traces of random walks across the inputted automata.

I wanna say these are the traces I am expecting to get out:

\[t_1 = \sigma_1, \lambda_1, \sigma_2, \lambda_2, \sigma_3, \lambda_3 \qquad \text{and} \qquad t_2 = \sigma_1, \lambda_1', \sigma_2', \lambda_2', \sigma_3', \lambda_3'\]

It should be noted, the trace output is in the SPOT format.

I want to do a test run of dot_trace_generator.py w/ the Kuhn Poker data to learn more about how this works.

Current Pipeline:

TSLF -> DOT Automaton / HOA File -> test_kuhn.py
